content.problemStatement:
Little Sajal went to school and learned how to calculate the score of an integer. The score of an integer is defined as the sum of the absolute value of the difference of all pairs of digits in this integer. For example, the score of 214 is ŌłŻ2ŌłÆ1ŌłŻ+ŌłŻ1ŌłÆ4ŌłŻ+ŌłŻ2ŌłÆ4ŌłŻ=6|2 - 1| + |1 - 4| + |2 - 4| = 6ŌłŻ2ŌłÆ1ŌłŻ+ŌłŻ1ŌłÆ4ŌłŻ+ŌłŻ2ŌłÆ4ŌłŻ=6.

As Sajal is very smart, he quickly learned it, and as an exercise, the teacher gave him a task. He will be given two integers LLL and RRR, and he will be asked to calculate the sum of scores of all integers between LLL and RRR (inclusive).

As this task is a little bit hard for Sajal, he wants your help.


Input Format

The first number will be the number of test cases TTT (TŌēż104T \leq 10^4TŌēż104).
Then there will be TTT lines, each line containing two integers LLL and RRR (1ŌēżLŌēżRŌēż10181 \leq L \leq R \leq 10^{18}1ŌēżLŌēżRŌēż1018).


Output Format

For each test case, your program should output the desired sum in a single line. As the sum can be very large, output it modulo 1000000007.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For the first test case, the score of 214 is ŌłŻ2ŌłÆ1ŌłŻ+ŌłŻ1ŌłÆ4ŌłŻ+ŌłŻ2ŌłÆ4ŌłŻ=6|2 - 1| + |1 - 4| + |2 - 4| = 6ŌłŻ2ŌłÆ1ŌłŻ+ŌłŻ1ŌłÆ4ŌłŻ+ŌłŻ2ŌłÆ4ŌłŻ=6.
For the second test case, the scores of 12, 13, 14, 15, and 16 are calculated and summed up to 15.
For the third test case, since there are no pairs of digits in single-digit numbers, the score is 0.
For the fourth test case, the scores of 257, 258, 259, and 260 are calculated and summed up to 48.
For the fifth test case, the score of 112 is ŌłŻ1ŌłÆ1ŌłŻ+ŌłŻ1ŌłÆ2ŌłŻ+ŌłŻ1ŌłÆ2ŌłŻ=2|1 - 1| + |1 - 2| + |1 - 2| = 2ŌłŻ1ŌłÆ1ŌłŻ+ŌłŻ1ŌłÆ2ŌłŻ+ŌłŻ1ŌłÆ2ŌłŻ=2.
